questions.question: Explain the concept of a programming paradigm and why it is important for a programmer.
keyPoints: A programming paradigm is a style or approach to structuring code
It defines how programmers think about problem-solving
Important for choosing the right approach for different problems
Helps in understanding different programming languages and their strengths

questions.question: Differentiate between Imperative and Declarative programming paradigms with examples.
keyPoints: Imperative describes HOW to accomplish a task through explicit commands
Declarative describes WHAT to accomplish rather than HOW
C is an example of imperative/procedural programming
SQL is an example of declarative programming

questions.question: List and briefly explain the four fundamental concepts of Object-Oriented Programming.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: bundling data and methods together
Inheritance: acquiring properties from parent class
Polymorphism: taking multiple forms
Abstraction: hiding complex details, showing essentials

questions.question: What is the procedural programming paradigm? How does it organize code?
keyPoints: Organizes code into procedures or functions
Each function performs a specific task
Promotes modularity and code reusability
C is a classic example of procedural language

questions.question: Explain why C is considered a middle-level programming language.
keyPoints: Combines features of both high-level and low-level languages
Provides low-level memory access through pointers
Offers high-level structured programming constructs
Allows direct memory manipulation like assembly but with readability of high-level languages

questions.question: What is the difference between a compiled language and an interpreted language?
keyPoints: Compiled languages translate entire code to machine code before execution
Interpreted languages execute code line-by-line at runtime
C is a compiled language
Compiled languages generally offer faster execution speed

questions.question: Define encapsulation and explain its advantages in software development.
keyPoints: Encapsulation bundles data and methods into a single unit (class)
Restricts direct access to some components
Prevents unintended interference with data
Enhances security and modularity

questions.question: Explain the Object-Oriented Programming paradigm in detail. Discuss its key features, advantages, and how it differs from procedural programming.
keyPoints: OOP organizes code around objects containing data and methods
Four fundamental features: Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, Abstraction
Advantages: code reusability, maintainability, scalability, real-world modeling
Difference from procedural: combines data and functions together vs separating them
Provides better problem modeling for complex systems

questions.question: Compare and contrast Functional Programming and Object-Oriented Programming paradigms. Discuss their characteristics and suitable use cases.
keyPoints: Functional: treats computation as evaluation of mathematical functions, avoids changing state
Object-Oriented: organizes around objects with data and methods
Functional advantages: easier debugging, parallel programming, predictable behavior
OOP advantages: better modeling of real-world entities, inheritance hierarchies, encapsulation
Functional suitable for mathematical computations, OOP for complex business applications

questions.question: Discuss the various programming paradigms. Include at least five paradigms with their characteristics, advantages, and at least one example language for each.
keyPoints: Imperative/Procedural: C, Pascal - step-by-step instructions, direct state modification
Object-Oriented: Java, C++, Python - objects with data and methods
Functional: Haskell, Lisp - pure functions, no side effects
Declarative: SQL, HTML - describe what not how
Logic: Prolog - facts and rules based on formal logic
Structured: emphasizes code organization using blocks and subroutines

questions.question: Explain the structure of a C program. How does C demonstrate procedural programming paradigm principles?
keyPoints: C program structure: preprocessor directives, main function, function declarations, variable declarations, statements
main() is the entry point of every C program
Functions organize code into reusable modules
C demonstrates procedural paradigm through functions and structured approach
Supports modular programming through separate compilation of functions
